title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A joint player, accessibility, security, quality, performance, packaging, and support review board. The board needs complete player-outcome evidence, barrier closure, bounded local data, immutable artifacts, accessibility and capacity gates, clean installs, staged release, corrupted-state recovery, rollback rehearsal, support ownership, and explicit residual-risk acceptance. Planned artifact: Deliver Asteroids Production and Recovery Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection,…
objectives: Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.
Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Acceptance Evidence
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Asteroids team handles case 3899 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Acceptance Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-production-defense-4tfyz2" that uses case 2476 to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-production-defense-4tfyz2
# Competency: asteroids-production-defense.
# Case 2476: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_production_defense_4tfyz2(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, performance: bool = True, portability: bool = True, security: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_4tfyz2 = "4tfyz2-2zyft4-2476"
    assert evidence_variant_4tfyz2
    evidence_axes_4tfyz2 = (
        "player4tfyz2",
        "seed2476",
        "input2zyft4",
        "frame4tfyz22476",
        "state24764tfyz2",
        "playtest2zyft42476",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_4tfyz2)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "player-quality"
    if not performance or not portability or not security:
        return False, passed, "release-quality"
    if not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "recovery-gap"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-game-defense"
